name: Assassin's Creed
description: Ubisoft's historical action-adventure game series, from the Holy Land to feudal Japan

questions.q: Which video game publisher develops the Assassin's Creed series?

source.notes: Ubisoft Montreal has led development since the original 2007 release.

questions.q: What is the name of the device that lets users relive the genetic memories of Assassin ancestors?

source.notes: Invented by Abstergo Industries based on Isu technology.

questions.q: In the modern-day storyline, which Templar-front corporation kidnaps and experiments on Desmond Miles?

source.notes: A multinational pharmaceutical/tech company controlled by the Templar Order.

questions.q: The Assassin Brotherhood is locked in a centuries-long war with which rival order?

source.notes: The series' foundational thematic conflict: freedom vs. order.

questions.q: In what year was the first Assassin's Creed game released?

source.notes: Released November 2007 for PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360.

questions.q: Who is the modern-day protagonist of the first three mainline Assassin's Creed games?

source.notes: A bartender descended from both Altaïr and Ezio.

questions.q: In Assassin's Creed IV: Black Flag, what lifestyle does protagonist Edward Kenway lead?

source.notes: Set in the Golden Age of Piracy in the Caribbean, 1715-1722.

questions.q: The first Assassin's Creed is set during which historical conflict?

source.notes: 1191 AD; features cities of Jerusalem, Acre, and Damascus.

questions.q: Who is the playable Assassin in Assassin's Creed II through Revelations?

source.notes: His trilogy spans Florence, Rome, and Constantinople.

questions.q: Assassin's Creed III stars which half-Mohawk, half-British Assassin?

source.notes: Set during the American Revolution; father Haytham was a Templar.

questions.q: Assassin's Creed Unity is primarily set during which historical event?

source.notes: Protagonist Arno Dorian pursues King Louis XVI's fate.

questions.q: Which 2015 Victorian London-set entry features twins Jacob and Evie Frye?

source.notes: Their gang, the Rooks, battles Blighters in industrial-era London.

questions.q: The 2017 game Assassin's Creed Origins stars Bayek of Siwa, a Medjay in which civilization?

source.notes: Bayek and Aya co-found the Hidden Ones, the Brotherhood's predecessor.

questions.q: In Assassin's Creed Odyssey (2018), players can choose between which two sibling Spartan mercenaries?

source.notes: Set during the Peloponnesian War, 431 BC.

questions.q: The 2020 Assassin's Creed Valhalla's story centers on a Viking warrior named what?

source.notes: A raider-turned-jarl who leads her clan to settle in 9th-century England.

questions.q: The Templar Grand Master Robert de Sable serves as the main antagonist of which AC game?

source.notes: A real historical figure who led the Knights Templar in the 1190s.

questions.q: Which Renaissance Pope is the primary antagonist of Assassin's Creed II?

source.notes: Grand Master of the Templar Order in this era.

questions.q: Who is the final Templar target Ezio pursues in Assassin's Creed: Brotherhood?

source.notes: Son of Rodrigo Borgia; thrown from the walls of Viana in the finale.

questions.q: Which Vladimir Bartol novel was a key inspiration for the series?

source.notes: Slovene novel (1938) about the medieval Order of Assassins.

questions.q: In Assassin's Creed Origins, Bayek and Aya co-found the Brotherhood under what early name?

source.notes: Later evolve into the Assassin Brotherhood centuries on.

questions.q: The 2023 entry Assassin's Creed Mirage is set primarily in which historic city?

source.notes: Set in 9th-century Baghdad during the Islamic Golden Age; protagonist Basim.

questions.q: Which First Civilization (Isu) member is released from imprisonment at the end of Assassin's Creed III?

source.notes: Her release sets up much of the modern-day plot through Syndicate.
